# baseline-v5 — openWakeWord native training pipeline

Trained "Hey Bubba" / "Heybubba" classifier using openWakeWord's own training
script. Same speech_embedding front-end as v4, but the classifier was trained
their way:

  * positives + adversarial negatives generated by piper-sample-generator
  * mixed against ~2000 hours of background speech features (ACAV100M dump)
  * augmented with real RIRs (openSLR-26) + noise (UrbanSound8K + MS-SNSD)
  * auto-tuned negative weighting to hit a target FPR

This produced a model that finally works — fires reliably on "Hey Bubba" /
"Heybubba", doesn't fire on silence or background speech.

## Reported training metrics (auto test set)
- Final Accuracy: 0.73
- Final Recall: 0.48
- Final FPR/hour: 0.62

The auto test set is harder than real-world because it includes adversarial
phoneme-overlap negatives; in practice the model behaves better than these
numbers suggest. **Validated on Mac mic — works much better than v1-v4.**

## Pipeline (matches v4 chain)
```
audio (16 kHz)
   │
   ▼  melspectrogram.onnx    (frozen, ~1.0 MB, from openWakeWord v0.5.1)
mel features (T, 32)
   │
   ▼  sliding 76-frame windows, stride 8 → 16 windows
   │
   ▼  embedding_model.onnx   (frozen, ~1.3 MB, from openWakeWord v0.5.1)
embeddings (16, 96)
   │
   ▼  hey_bubba_v0.1.onnx    (trained here, ~200 KB)
score (1,) sigmoid output
```

Note the **16** embedding count — different from our v4 attempt which used 15.

## Training config highlights
- 5000 positive train + 1000 positive test (piper-sample-generator TTS for "hey bubba" + "heybubba")
- ~36000 adversarial-negative phoneme-overlap clips
- 2000 hours background speech features (ACAV100M)
- 30000 train steps, auto-tuned negative_weight to hit target_fpr=0.2
- Model: 2-layer DNN (layer_size=32) + sigmoid head

## Files
- `exports/melspectrogram.onnx` — frozen openWakeWord front-end
- `exports/embedding_model.onnx` — frozen openWakeWord speech embedding
- `exports/hey_bubba_v0.1.onnx` — our trained classifier (~200 KB)
- `training_config.yaml` — the heybubba.yaml used to produce this
- `whistle-demo-v0.14.0-trained.apk` — Android test app (added below)
